summary: Do not specify negative limit in FindOne operation
description:
With the 1.3.0 upgrade, the findOne test within PHPLIB began triggering this assertion failure in libmongoc:

src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-cursor.c:52 _mongoc_n_return(): precondition failed: remaining > 0

Dumping the Query object in the PHP driver reported int(4294967295) as the limit (largest possible 32-bit int), which jives with libmongoc using a uint32_t for the cursor limit and PHPLIB specifying -1 for its FindOne limit. IIRC, -1 and 1 are both handled the same by the server (the cursor is immediately closed), so it's a trivial fix in PHPLIB to use 1 instead of -1.

summary: Add global typemap configuration
description: Right now, the default typemap is to return a stdClass object for documents. You can only set a typemap for each specific query/iterations. We should add a driver specific connection option to allow for a typemap that is the default typemap to be applied for *all* deserialisations paths from BSON to PHP variables.

summary: Improve _id extraction for Serializable objects
description:
When inserting objects, {{BulkWrite::insert()}} only returns an ID if it needed to be generated. Otherwise, the userland library falls back to attempting to extra the ID from the inserted document, which may be an array or object.

Objects implementing the BSON Serializable interface may not have a public {{_id}} property, which may lead to either of two error messages:

 * Undefined property: CLASSNAME::$_id
 * Cannot access protected property ::$_id

While PHPC-382 will ultimately resolve this by changing {{BulkWrite::insert()}} to always return the inserted ID (not only when generated), a temporary workaround for Serializable instances requires that we invoke {{bsonSerialize()}} from userland and extract an "_id" key from the returned array or object.

summary: Use type map for command responses
description: Currently, the drop and generic command helper methods do not apply a type map to the cursor. We should be strict about always applying the type map to any cursors given back to the user (this would exclude the ones we use to internally construct iterators for database, collection, and index enumeration).

summary: Use explicit database and collection name arguments in Collection ctor
description: The Collection constructor was the only place where we were soliciting a namespace (e.g. "db.collection") string argument. For consistency, let's split these up to explicit database and collection name arguments.

summary: 404 error for tutorial
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/releases/tag/1.0.0
"Installation Instructions for the HHVM driver may be found in the PHP.net tutorial."

-> http://php.net/manual/en/mongodb.tutorial.install.hhvm.php
404 error

summary: Unknown Operator $id when using MongoId query
description:
Any use of a MongoId for queries results in the following error:

ConnectionException in Find.php line 179: unknown operator: $id

    in Find.php line 179
    at Server->executeQuery('debug.debug_logs', object(Query), object(ReadPreference)) in Find.php line 179
    at Find->execute(object(Server)) in FindOne.php line 81
    at FindOne->execute(object(Server)) in Collection.php line 468
    at Collection->findOne(array('_id' => object(MongoId))) in routes.php line 70

summary: Don't apply type map option to non-cursor aggregate results
Fix merging of cursorType, comment, and maxTimeMS options for Find operation
Bucket::delete() should not delete orphaned chunks before files document
Bucket::rename() should use updateOne() and $set instead of replaceOne()
description:
Per the spec:

bq. Drivers construct and execute an update_one command on the files collection using { _id: @id } as the filter and { $set : { filename : "new_filename" } } as the update parameter.

summary: Fix merging of comment and maxTimeMS options
Windows install reports errors and fails
description:
Here is the command and error messages:

summary: Ensure GridFS removal doesn't leave orphan chunks behind
description:
PHP-1228 describes an edge case with the 1.x driver's method for removing files:

bq. Because this isn't a transaction, it is possible that a new file gets inserted while another process is in the middle of MongoGridFS::remove(). And if that new file happens to match the removal criteria, we could end up deleting its fs.files document and leaving its chunks behind. That said, it looks like you were only removing fs.files documents in your OP, so I don't think this would be an issue.

summary: GridFS implementation should support files larger than 4GB
Support non-ObjectID identifiers for GridFS files
description:
The updated [GridFS Spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst] adds two new methods:
* open_upload_stream_with_id
* upload_from_stream_with_id

Both need to be implemented and tested.

description:

summary: listIndexes() returns empty object
Exceptions during StreamWrapper IO should trigger errors and return false
Unset typeMap key before passing driverOptions to Manager constructor
description:
Currently, we pass {{$driverOptions}} with a "typeMap" key to the driver, which ignores it; however, the unsupported option will impose on the driver's incorporating that constructor argument in the hash for persistent connections.

Unset it so that we only pass supported options to the driver.

summary: FindAndModify writeConcern option is sent as empty document
description:
The implementation for PHPLIB-129 sets a MongoDB\Driver\WriteConcern as a field in the findAndModify command document. The object is serialized as an empty BSON document since it does not implement a {{get_properties}} handler, nor does it have a {{bsonSerialize()}} method (before PHPC-498).

Contrast this with PHPLIB-130, which uses a helper method to convert a MongoDB\Driver\ReadConcern to a PHP object before setting it within a command document.

As an aside, this bug exposed an issue with the implementation for PHPC-498 (tracking in PHPC-801).

summary: Pass UTCDateTime constructor argument as integer
description: [ca19876|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/commit/ca19876ca97a1d916441f4435cb7e3d9612bc1e2] (for HHVM-130) removed the default {{(string)}} cast from the UTCDateTime constructor. This issue does not present itself with the PHP extension, as the constructor argument is currently parsed as a string (as of PHPC-726).

summary: Default Client URI to "mongodb://127.0.0.1/"
description: As of PHPC-746 and HHVM-237, {{MongoDB\Driver\Manager}}'s constructor use libmongoc's default of {{"mongodb://127.0.0.1/"}} for the URI. The library should either use this same string or {{null}} to defer to the driver's default.

summary: how can i set find().limit() in this library
description:
how can i set a limit rows in with this library
$mongo->find()->limit() not found in the documentation and not exist in the library methods

summary: Missing CONTRIBUTING.md
description: We should list the immediate plan with the library and call for contributions, feedback, and creation of new libraries.

summary: Remind users that connection URIs should be encoded
description: Per the [connection string specification|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/connection-string/connection-string-spec.rst], URI components should be encoded to facilitate proper parsing. This may differ from the legacy driver, which did not fully implement the specification.

summary: Unpack MongoDB\BSON\Serializable instances before reading properties
description:
{{get_object_vars()}} returns an empty array for ArrayObject instances (e.g. MongoDB\Model\BSONDocument). This is problematic if such an object is used for an index specification or replacement document.

We should check if an object implements MongoDB\BSON\Serializable and use the return value of its {{bsonSerialize()}} method if so. That is currently done for {{extract_id_from_inserted_document()}}.

summary: ReadConcern default should only be set when supported by the server
description: The Collection's ReadConcern should only be applied as a default option value to operations when it is supported by the selected server. This is consistent with PHPLIB-200 for WriteConcern application.

summary: FindAndModify operations should not accept unsupported write concern options
Operations should not accept unsupported read concern options
description: Per the [ReadConcern specification|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/read-write-concern/read-write-concern.rst#errors-1], it is an error to specify a ReadConcern option when not supported by the selected server.

summary: Configurable type map for findAndModify and non-cursor aggregate commands
description: When using FindAndModify, or other commands in which we return document data (e.g. non-cursor aggregate, distinct), users should have control over the BSON deserialization.

summary: GridFS should delete orphan chunks for failed uploads
description:
Per the [GridFS specification|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst]:

{quote}
*Aborting an upload*

Drivers SHOULD provide a mechanism to abort an upload. When using open_upload_stream, the returned Stream SHOULD have an Abort method. When using upload_from_stream, the upload will be aborted if the source stream raises an error.

When an upload is aborted any chunks already uploaded MUST be deleted. Note that this differs from the case where an attempt to insert a chunk fails, in which case drivers immediately report the failure without attempting to delete any chunks already uploaded.

Abort MUST raise an error if it is unable to succesfully abort the upload (for example, if an error occurs while deleting any chunks already uploaded). However, if the upload is being aborted because the source stream provided to upload_from_stream raised an error then the original error should be re-raised.

Abort MUST also close the Stream, or at least place it in an aborted state, so any further attempts to write additional content to the Stream after Abort has been called fail immediately.
{quote}

PHP's StreamWrapper has no API for aborting an upload; however, we should still follow the abort process if uploading a file encounters an error.

summary: Use strings instead of memory stream for GridFS upload buffering
description: Based on benchmarks, strings will be more performant than memory streams. This should also simplify the write buffering logic in the WritableStream class.

summary: Require chunkSizeBytes to be a positive integer
description: We currently only validate that "chunkSizeBytes" is an integer without checking its range. Using a value < 1 could lead to a non-terminating loop in WritableStream.

summary: Rephrase unsupported/corrupt BSON messages
Can't copy & paste link ref. in doc details, it collaps on click!
description:
E.g. see screendump

http://mongodb.github.io/mongo-php-library/api/class-MongoDB.Collection.html

summary: GridFS should report file mode using stat(2) bits
GridFS stat should report modified and created timestamps
GridFS stat should report chunk size in blksize field
description:
According to the [{{stat()}} documentation|http://php.net/manual/en/function.stat.php], {{blksize}} is the "blocksize of filesystem IO". This corresponds to the GridFS chunk size.

The {{blocks}} field is always the "number of 512-byte blocks allocated" and has no direct relationship with {{blksize}} (see: [this documentation|https://www.gnu.org/software/libc/manual/html_node/Attribute-Meanings.html]). While we could easily calculate this value, it's not relevant to GridFS and users might be confused in thinking that it is actually the number of GridFS chunks. We should continue to leave it unset (i.e. 0).

summary: WritableStream::getSize() should include buffer length
description:
Currently, WriteableStream only considers inserted chunks when reporting its file length. The buffer size should also be reported, since files can only be appended and that data will ultimately be inserted into the next chunk (assuming an exception does not occur).

When seeking is implemented (PHPLIB-213), this will make the file's reported size consistent with its position from [{{ftell()}}|http://php.net/ftell].

summary: Use strings instead of memory stream for GridFS download buffering
description: Similar to refactoring for upload streams in PHPLIB-244, this should simplify logic in the ReadableStream class.

summary: Duplicate server selection in Collection::findOne()
description: For PHPLIB-228, [4c61d3c|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/4c61d3c5aecfb0ae8a4958806d64db1d66bca05e#diff-810cdcfdd8a6b9e1fc0d1e96d7786874R462] added an earlier server selection in {{MongoDB\Collection::findOne()}} but neglected to remove the original server selection before the operation is executed. As such, it's possible for the driver to select a secondary that supports read concerns, inject a default read concern, and then execute the operation on an older secondary that does not support read concerns, thereby triggering an error (as described [here|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/issues/326#issuecomment-280304336]).

summary: Omit default read and write concern from commands
description:
Quoting a comment from [mongodb/mongo-php-library#326|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/issues/326#issuecomment-273516889]:

bq. If the library can have a notion of an unset read concern, read preference, and write concern, that would allow us to not pass that around the selected objects (e.g. Database, Collection, Bucket) and inject it into each operation.

Currently, the library selects the read concern, read preference, and write concern after a {{MongoDB\Driver\Manager}} is constructed and passes that amongst its classes. As such, those classes have no way of knowing if the values were specified by a user or the driver's defaults. This is relevant for things like write concern, where the driver need not include a default write concern in commands.

There are some implications for libmongoc here. Although it always omits applying a default read concern (i.e. "{}") to queries, based on [3e34af3|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/3e34af30faca57e014044e0a3b7ca8f3460991df] (for CDRIVER-1527), it lacks equivalent logic for write concerns. CDRIVER-1322 included a function to detect default write concerns, but logic in [3e34af3|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/3e34af30faca57e014044e0a3b7ca8f3460991df#diff-f056549fad972eba9c93b7e0dcd9542bR1414] (also for CDRIVER-1527) appears to include a default write concern so long as the option is specified. Since PHPLIB currently always provides a write concern to commands, we do see it sent across the wire:

{noformat}
2017-01-17T16:40:59.966-0500 I COMMAND  [conn1] command test.foo command: insert { insert: "foo", writeConcern: {}, ordered: true, documents: [ { x: 1, _id: ObjectId('587e8f6b6118fd4a0c5acbf2') } ] } ninserted:1 keysInserted:1 numYields:0 reslen:44 locks:{ Global: { acquireCount: { r: 3, w: 3 } }, Database: { acquireCount: { w: 2, W: 1 } }, Collection: { acquireCount: { w: 2 } } } protocol:op_query 3ms
...
2017-01-17T16:45:33.064-0500 I COMMAND  [conn2] command test.foo command: findAndModify { findAndModify: "foo", new: false, upsert: false, query: { x: 1 }, update: { $set: { y: 1 } }, writeConcern: {} } planSummary: COLLSCAN update: { $set: { y: 1 } } keysExamined:0 docsExamined:1 nMatched:1 nModified:1 numYields:0 reslen:120 locks:{ Global: { acquireCount: { r: 1, w: 1 } }, Database: { acquireCount: { w: 1 } }, Collection: { acquireCount: { w: 1 } } } protocol:op_query 0ms
{noformat}

summary: Allow empty pipeline array for Collection::aggregate()
description: [mongodb/mongo-php-library#398|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/issues/398] inquired why we currently throw an exception if the pipeline array is empty. The server does not require a non-empty array.

summary: Rely on BulkWrite::insert() to always return the document's ID
description: With PHPC-471, we can remove help functions to extract the inserted document's ID in userland.

summary: Count should not convert hint document to an index name string
description: If the {{hint}} option is a key pattern document (i.e. array or object), it should be passed to the {{count}} command as-is. The library currently converts documents to an index name string, which can cause a "bad hint" server error if the index was created with a custom name and cannot be found.

summary: Cache results for info iterators that decorate a Cursor
description: Some of the legacy info iterators operate on inline results, which mean they can be rewound and counted multiple times. The same is not true for the iterators that wrap a Cursor (be it a command cursor or query cursor on a system collection). Using a CachingIterator (or something similar) when necessary would keep this consistent.

summary: Test suite should not query featureCompatibilityVersion for server versions before 3.4
description:
The [{{getFeatureCompatibilityVersion()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/d1cd6efa8bd65f93f543f5e76a5cee20a4fcc6b9#diff-a4c0b94cff2649664fe18feb5a1ac485R79] test function introduced for PHPLIB-276 should only be called if the server version is 3.4+, as earlier versions of the database will raise an error a ["no option found to get"|https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-library/jobs/310121820#L4863] error if we query the server for its FCV.

Alternatively, we may want to rewrite the {{getFeatureCompatibilityVersion()}} so that is not simply a direct comparison of the FCV parameter. We can first do a server version check, and if the server is 3.2 or older, we can use the server version as the FCV for comparison and stop there. Otherwise, we query for the FCV parameter on 3.4+ servers and use _that_ version for the comparison.

summary: Quering with $in  of ObjectIds not working as expected
Handle MongoDB\BSON\Serializable $out argument for Collection::mapReduce()
description: {{Collection::mapReduce()}} checks if the {{$out}} argument indicates inline output by examining the first key of the object/array. It would be helpful if it also supported {{MongoDB\BSON\Serializable}} objects, as [{{is_first_key_operator()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/blob/68287e92bb756e56ba847e9f988d8caf1d67eda6/src/functions.php#L91] does.
